skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Design distinctive, production-ready frontend interfaces with strong typography, layout, motion, and responsive polish.
skill.summary: Design memorable web interfaces with production-ready frontend quality.
skill.author: AI-Vibe-Prompts

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and one network indicator. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, CSS and JSX examples, ordinary design guidance, and an inline SVG data URL namespace. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, exfiltration, or executable helper script was found.

low_findings.title: Static command execution detections are false positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fenced code blocks and inline code examples, not executable skill logic. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: line-number review shows CSS, JSX, and Markdown content only, with no shell invocation path or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backtick matches are Markdown syntax around examples and integration names. They do not execute commands or construct shell input.

low_findings.title: Static weak cryptography detections are false positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported high-severity blocker locations contain metadata, headings, design terms, CSS examples, and checklist text, not cryptographic APIs or weak algorithms. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: manual review found no hashing, encryption, random number generation, or password-handling logic in the cited file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is prose and frontend presentation examples. No cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive implementation is present.

low_findings.title: Static reconnaissance detections are false positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported reconnaissance locations are design-planning prompts and layout guidance such as audience, constraints, positioning, and visual composition. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: these lines do not query the host, inspect environment details, list files, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines discuss frontend design context and visual techniques. There is no system reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Static network URL detection is a false positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The URL at line 242 appears inside an inline SVG data URL as the SVG XML namespace, not as a network request target. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: the surrounding CSS embeds a data:image value for a noise texture, and no fetch, beacon, import, or external resource load is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is part of SVG namespace metadata inside a data URI. It is not used to transmit data or retrieve remote content.

content.value_statement: Many generated interfaces look generic and fail to match the product context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with stronger visual direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection of distinctive typography, color, layout, and motion direction.
Provides frontend aesthetics principles for pages, components, and applications.
Encourages responsive, accessible, and performant implementation standards.
Includes examples for CSS animation, atmospheric backgrounds, and React motion patterns.
Defines a quality checklist for visual polish before delivery.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, build tooling, or automated design validation.
Does not generate image assets or font files by itself.
Requires the agent to adapt examples to the project framework and design system.
May need additional accessibility and performance testing for production releases.

use_cases.title: Launch a polished marketing page
use_cases.description: Create a landing page with a clear visual direction, responsive layout, and memorable motion details.

use_cases.title: Upgrade an application screen
use_cases.description: Improve an existing product interface with stronger hierarchy, better spacing, and more intentional styling.

use_cases.title: Build a standout portfolio piece
use_cases.description: Design a showcase page that avoids generic templates and highlights distinctive frontend craft.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple page direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new page concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to design a responsive landing page for [product]. Choose a clear aesthetic direction and explain the main visual choices.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an existing interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a redesign pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the frontend-design skill to improve this screen: [describe screen]. Keep the current functionality, but refine typography, spacing, color, and motion.

prompt_templates.title: Build a production component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a production-ready [component type] for [audience]. Include responsive behavior, accessible states, and polished interaction details.

prompt_templates.title: Define a full visual system
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a broader product surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to define a visual direction for [application]. Cover layout strategy, typography, palette, motion principles, and quality checks.

output_examples.input: Design a landing page for a privacy-focused analytics tool.
output_examples.output: A refined dark interface with editorial typography, restrained accent colors, sharp data previews, and a clear responsive structure.

output_examples.input: Refresh a dashboard that feels generic.
output: A stronger hierarchy for metrics and navigation.
A focused palette with one clear accent color.
Motion used only for meaningful state changes.

output_examples.input: Create a portfolio homepage for a motion designer.
output_examples.output: A visually expressive homepage with bold type, layered project previews, responsive composition, and controlled animation moments.

best_practices: Start by choosing one clear aesthetic direction before writing interface code.
Pair distinctive visual choices with accessibility, performance, and responsive requirements.
Use the checklist to review polish, hierarchy, motion, and production readiness.
anti_patterns: Using the same generic fonts, purple gradients, and card layouts for every project.
Adding animation that distracts from task completion or reduces accessibility.
Copying example snippets without adapting them to the product context.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create polished frontend pages, components, and applications with a distinctive visual direction.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable scripts?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill with examples and quality criteria.

faq.question: Can it work with React projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance includes React and motion examples, but the ideas can apply to other frontend stacks.

faq.question: Does it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It can guide visual direction, but project teams should still follow established design system rules.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no executable code, malicious instructions, or data exfiltration behavior.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Users should provide the product purpose, audience, brand constraints, target framework, and accessibility requirements.
